pairs.question: Who was Gerard FitzGerald and what position did he play in hurling?
pairs.answer: Gerard FitzGerald was an Irish hurler born on 24 April 1964 and passed away on 17 March 2025. He played as a right corner-forward for the Cork senior hurling team.

pairs.question: What notable achievements did Gerard FitzGerald have during his hurling career?
pairs.answer: FitzGerald won two All-Ireland medals, three Munster medals, one national league medal in 1993, and a Railway Cup medal in 1992 with the Munster inter-provincial team. At club level, he won one All-Ireland medal with Midleton, two Munster medals, and four championship medals.

pairs.question: How many championship appearances did Gerard FitzGerald make throughout his career?
pairs.answer: Throughout his hurling career, Gerard FitzGerald made 16 championship appearances.

pairs.question: When did Gerard FitzGerald make his senior hurling debut for Cork?
pairs.answer: Gerard FitzGerald made his senior debut for Cork during the 1983–84 league season.

pairs.question: What roles did Gerard FitzGerald take on after retiring from playing hurling?
pairs.answer: After retiring from playing, FitzGerald became involved in team management and coaching. He served as either manager or selector with the Cork minor, under-21, and senior teams, as well as being involved with several club teams.

pairs.question: What is one tribute paid to Gerard FitzGerald after his death?
pairs.answer: After his death on 17 March 2025, Taoiseach Micheál Martin paid tribute to Gerard FitzGerald.

pairs.question: Which club did Gerard FitzGerald represent, and what success did he achieve there?
pairs.answer: Gerard FitzGerald represented Midleton at club level. He achieved success by winning one All-Ireland medal, two Munster medals, and four championship medals with the club.
